title: lomatium triternatum var. anomalum
intro: Lomatium triternatum var. anomalum, also known as Great Basin Lomatium or Anomalus, is a perennial plant native to the Great Basin region of North America, characterized by its fern-like foliage and yellow or white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Lomatium triternatum var. anomalum include its ability to support immune function, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, such as congestion and cough. Herbal preparations of Lomatium triternatum var. anomalum can be made into tinctures, teas, and salves, and are often used to support overall health and well-being.
